description: Architecture-to-UX design: discover/resolve input → parse architecture → parse blueprint → clarify → skill-operator UX → target-software UX → user stories → surface-specific UX → error/recovery UX → E2E scenario seeds → architecture feedback → final document → quality-gate self-check. The fourth stage of research-pipeline → blueprint → architecture → ux-design → implementation-plan, consuming an architecture design document (architecture --mode design output) and optionally the matching blueprint and tech-stack. It is a user-story-driven experience design skill, not a screen-drawing skill: it separates Skill Operator UX (how the user drives the skill workflow) from Target Software UX (how end users and agents interact with the product), and its highest-value output is testable user stories, failure/recovery UX, E2E scenario seeds, and mandatory architecture feedback (recommending architecture --mode reconcile when UX exposes architecture gaps). The composed document has 22 sections plus an Appendix A UX Quality-Gate Self-Check. Hybrid is the default operating mode (ask only high-impact UX questions, infer low-risk details, record assumptions). Pure-LLM transformation: every task is a reasoning step driven by a prompt file; there is no deterministic CLI/MCP backend. The manifest is valid as documentation for a prompt-driven agent and ready for a future runtime runner; if no runner exists, follow the task order manually. ux-design never re-decides architecture or the tech stack, never writes executable tests, and never produces pixel-level visual design. v0.3.0 phase-and-testability-metadata pass: user-story prompt (07) requires a phase/release-gate header on every story (Phase: MVP-0/MVP-1/Phase 2/Phase 3/Future, Primary Surface, Release Gate, Depends On); E2E seed prompt (10) requires a testability metadata block on every seed (phase, surface, release gate, deterministic, requires real LLM, CI suitable, required fixtures, must mock, required architecture contracts, required implementation components) plus a Testability Summary Table in §20; the e2e-scenario and user-story templates are updated accordingly; quality-gate prompt (13) adds six new gate rows, five new fail conditions, and six new warning conditions; SKILL.md gains gate 11. v0.2.0 cross-skill-artifact-contract pass: the ux-design template conforms to the shared Cross-Skill Artifact Contract (references/artifact-contract.md) — §1 Generation Metadata carries Artifact Type (ux_design) + a stable Topic Slug; a Cross-Skill Artifact Contract block exposes Source Artifacts Consumed, Resolved Input Artifacts, and a Contract Field Map; and Appendix A includes a Cross-Skill Artifact Contract Gate. The final-document prompt instructs contract compliance with the controlled vocabulary.
